Sarnak Battle Shield  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 25
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 STA: +5 HP: +40
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD DRU BRD SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 25
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 STA: +5 HP: +40
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10

Item Type:Shield
Stackable:No

Tribute:480
Submitted By:Kaymara Mistwood
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-17 07:05:56
Page Updated:Sun Dec 27th, 2020

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 2,500pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 45
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Chardok
NPC Name
a Di`Zok Cryptmaster
a Di`Zok Legionnaire
a Di`zok Underling
a Reanimated Berserker
a Reanimated Partisan
a Shai`din Nightfear
An Iksar Trustee
Overseer Dal`guur

Halls of Betrayal (Chardok B)
NPC Name
a Shai`Din royal guard
a Shai`Din scythemaster


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Chardok
Halls of Betrayal (Chardok B)

# Jun 08 2014 at 7:26 PM Rating: Good
criblood
Scholar
22 posts
A Shai'din Nightfear. Seems like it can pop on any sarnak. It makes it much easier to find if you look for the sarnak with a equipped shield as it does show. Great twink shield :)
Edit: I was in plain ol Chardok. Don't know about Chardok B

# Apr 13 2004 at 9:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591800596180

These go for at least 4.5-5k. But then, everything on SZ is much more expensive than on other servers, with the exception of weight-reducing bags, which seem to be about the same price everywhere.

Regardless of the server, this makes for excellent twinkage.

Sarnak Battle Shield
QuoteReply
# May 28 2002 at 4:31 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253769762
56 posts
The suggested price of 7k listed here on the pic
is way outdated. I haveen't seen this sold for more than 2 k in over a month, on 3 diff servers. If
anyone is paying more than 2 k for it your getting ripped off --it's become way to common. Just look
around and see how many players have it equipped
▲ Page top
A Great Shield
QuoteReply
# May 07 2002 at 1:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771595041
8 posts
A wonderful Upgrade to Charred Guardian Shield that Ive been caring since I was level 20 lol. The AC and the Resists rock, and its light weight. Above all that its got a str bonus and for us High Elf Clerics that comes in REAL HANDY!!!

Lathandria Morninglady
47 cleric of Tunare
Officer of Sovereign Circle

http://www.magelo.com/eq_view_profile.html?num=179037

▲ Page top
RE: A Great Shield
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2002 at 11:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778672049
2 posts
I must agree with using this shield to upgrade. I personally went from the Charred guardian to a Crystal Chitin. I hated having to get rid of the +9 Wisdom, but now that I'm attending raids I need the resists and AC more. Great shield, and since I'm fashion conscious, it looks great with the surveyor's rod I have in primary.

Iumanya Illuminarii
46th Season High Elf Cleric of the Epic Court of Courage.
Luclin Server
▲ Page top
bah to noobs
QuoteReply
# Apr 28 2002 at 4:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591775354360
8 posts
To all the noobs who think all druids that use this are stupid, you simply don't know what you are talking about. Not only is this shield good, it is great! The reason it is so good is because of its usefullness to more then one class. It is good for druids (who wouldn't want AC and resist past 50?) and also non dual-weidling hybrids (shadowknights and pallies) that happen to use a 1h weapon. Also, it is good for bards with high-end instruments that can be used in primary if they are instrument fighting. This would be great for my bard when pulling, using drums of the beast or lute of the howler in combat, on a raid and not meleeing, or charm/swarm kiting. This is a relatively common, sweet-looking, low weight, amazing shield. Bah to noobs who say otherwise
▲ Page top
RE: bah to noobs
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2002 at 10:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755789514
61 posts
To the bards part, Most would just stand there going hand to hand. To get your other instruments out to properly instrument fight, You need to get the shield out to use the non-1hand instruments :)

That was then, this is now...
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2002 at 2:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770191902
Scholar
92 posts
Just curious, having read through the comments/arguments below, and I am now curious, since they changed the way mana is calculated over 200 now, ..... what is everyone's take on this now?

I have a while before my Druid gets up this high, but want to know what direction to plan for. My guess is that resists are still important, but since the change, has it made WIS more important?

Appreciate any and all insights and comments, and will cheerfully ignore useless flames.

Just got one
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 10:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248758846
View User Forum
161 posts
I just got one of these last night...I had to strip my Bard to do so..I traded all her visable armour(full Lambent, SS Gauntlets and SS Boots)for this shield for Eberk..Since I am going to be on Eberk full time I can always get Haarmony more armour..Her armour was worth about 6k and I see this shield being sold in EC for up to 10k so I guess it was a nice deal..notice a difference since I got the shield...cause when I agro,I mean heal :o) the extra ac and hp comes in handy..well good hunting and safe journey to all.
